appStoreConnect.subtitle: One Thought. One Day. Forever.
appStoreConnect.promotionalText: Write one sentence a day. Seal it. Rediscover it. Local weekly reflections, time capsules, and 1,400+ prompts.
appStoreConnect.whatsNew: Write one thought per day with guided prompts, time capsule reveals, and on-device weekly reflections with a philosopher guide. Free to start; premium adds reflections, photo journaling, mood tracking, and custom prompts.
appStoreConnect.description:
One thought a day. That's all it takes.

OnePrompt is a mindful journal for a small daily practice. Write one sentence, seal it for later, and rediscover who you were.

— WRITE LESS. REMEMBER MORE —
Daily prompts, time capsules, streaks, and nine colour themes keep journaling simple.

— PREMIUM: ON-DEVICE WEEKLY REFLECTIONS —
See a local weekly summary, theme, mood, encouragement, and photo count. Choose a philosopher guide — Nietzsche, Marcus Aurelius, Plato, Schiller, or Camus — and journaling goals to select curated variants. Your journal content stays on your device.

— PREMIUM: PHOTO JOURNALING, MOOD TRACKING, AND CUSTOM PROMPTS —
Capture a daily photo, log moods, and add your own prompts.

inAppPurchases.description: On-device weekly reflections, philosopher guides, photo journaling, mood tracking, custom prompts, fireplace background, background music, PDF export
All premium features at £1.67/month — best value

appStoreConnect.reviewNotes: OnePrompt is a personal journaling app. Users write one sentence per day with curated prompts and can seal entries as time capsules. Premium weekly reflections are deterministic and calculated on-device from the week's entries, selected goals, and philosopher guide; no journal content is sent to a reflection provider. Photo reflections count photo entries without inspecting image content. Photo journaling uses the device camera with explicit permission. RevenueCat handles subscriptions. The app works offline; cloud sync is optional and requires sign-in.

dataCollected.description: Email address for account creation and password recovery
Journal entries and photos stored on-device and optionally synced to cloud
Usage events via PostHog linked to a pseudonymous user UUID via posthog.identify() — no email or name collected
Crash reports via Sentry for stability improvements — no PII
